title: 2007年6月大学英语六级真题(第1套)

sections.title: Part I Writing
sections.directions: For this part, you are allowed 30 minutes to write a short essay entitled Should One Expect a Reward When Doing a Good Deed? You should write at least 150 words following the outline given below. 1. 有人做好事期望得到回报； 2. 有人认为应该像雷锋那样做好事不图回报； 3. 我的观点。 Should One Expect a Reward When Doing a Good Deed?

questions.explanation: A) Surfing the net. B) Watching a talk show. C) Packing a birthday gift. D) Shopping at a jewelry store.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) Surfing the net. B) Watching a talk show. C) Packing a birthday gift. D) Shopping at a jewelry store.

options.A: He enjoys finding fault with exams.
options.B: He is sure of his success in the exam.
options.C: He doesn’t know if he can do well in the exam.
options.D: He used to get straight A’s in the exams he took.

options.A: The man is generous with his good comments on people.
options.B: The woman is unsure if there will be peace in the world.
options.C: The woman is doubtful about newspaper stories.
options.D: The man is quite optimistic about human nature.

questions.explanation: A) Study for some profession. B) Attend a medical school. C) Stay in business. D) Sell his shop.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) Study for some profession. B) Attend a medical school. C) Stay in business. D) Sell his shop.

questions.explanation: A) More money. B) Fair treatment. C) A college education. D) Shorter work hours.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) More money. B) Fair treatment. C) A college education. D) Shorter work hours.

options.A: She was exhausted from her trip.
options.B: She missed the comforts of home.
options.C: She was impressed by Mexican food.
options.D: She will not go to Mexico again.

questions.explanation: A) Cheer herself up a bit. B) Find a more suitable job. C) Seek professional advice. D) Take a psychology course.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) Cheer herself up a bit. B) Find a more suitable job. C) Seek professional advice. D) Take a psychology course.

options.B: What he wears does not match his position.
options.C: He has ignored his friends since graduation.
options.D: He failed to do well at college.
To join in a training program.

questions.explanation: A) To go sightseeing. B) To have meetings. C) To promote a new champagne. D) To join in a training program.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) To go sightseeing. B) To have meetings. C) To promote a new champagne. D) To join in a training program.

options.A: It can reduce the number of passenger complaints.
options.B: It can make air travel more entertaining.
options.C: It can cut down the expenses for air travel.
options.D: It can lessen the discomfort caused by air travel.

options.A: Many of them found it difficult to exercise on a plane.
options.B: Many of them were concerned with their well-being.
options.C: Not many of them chose to do what she did.
options.D: Not many of them understood the program.

questions.explanation: A) At a fair. B) At a cafeteria. C) In a computer lab. D) In a shopping mall.

answerMeta.sourceText: A) At a fair. B) At a cafeteria. C) In a computer lab. D) In a shopping mall.

options.D: The dramatic changes in the job market.

questions.explanation: A) Data collection. B) Training consultancy. C) Corporate management. D) Information processing. Section B Directions: In this section, you will hear 3 short passages. At the end of each passage, you will hear some questions. Both the passage and the questions will be spoken only once. After you hear a question, you must choose the best answer from the four choices marked A), B), C) and D). Then mark the corresponding letter on Answer Sheet 2 with a single line through the centre. Passage One Questions 26 to 28 are based on the passage you have just heard.
